items.title: 'Appalling' lack of response to suspected overdose rates at Crown, expert says

children.content: Paramedics have attended more than 50 suspected overdoses at Crown Melbourne in the past two years, with the casino complex averaging an ambulance call-out for a suspected overdose every two weeks, data released exclusively to the ABC shows.

gallery.altText: One of the main entrances to Crown Casino in Melbourne.

thumbnail.altText: One of the main entrances to Crown Casino in Melbourne.

items.title: The Liberals said John was dead, but he's considering a new political life

children.content: When John Ternel quit the Victorian Liberal Party this week the party mistakenly sent an email to members saying he had died. Mr Ternel says the ordeal confirmed to him that the Victorian Liberals are in shambles.

image.altText: A man in a light business shirt and dark jacket looks into camera, with office buildings behind him.

items.title: Shed weight, go slow and pump up your tyres to save on fuel

children.content: How to reduce your petrol bill by changing your driving technique and tinkering with your car.

image.altText: A fuel nozzle in a car's fuel filler inlet.

items.title: Former top barrister spared jail after trying to overcharge clients

children.content: Norman O'Bryan was one of Melbourne's top barristers — until he was caught trying to overcharge his clients in a scandal described as "one of the darkest chapters" in Victorian legal history.

image.altText: A man in a suit leaves court

items.title: Bosisto's to triple eucalyptus oil production with new distillery

children.content: A 174-year-old Australian company is trying to address a decline in local eucalyptus oil production by opening a new distillery in central Victoria.

image.altText: A woman in a white shirt stands outside in front a eucalyptus oil distillery shed

items.title: Research links seismic testing to decreased commercial fish catch

children.content: New research links seismic testing used for oil and gas exploration with decreases in commercial fish catch rates in Victoria's east.

gallery.altText: An aerial shot of a vessel towing devices on the surface.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial shot of a vessel towing devices on the surface.

items.title: Calls for more education following the suspected death of 'Sammy' the seal

children.content: Marine experts are calling for greater education on aquatic life following the death of a seal believed to be known by Mornington Peninsula locals as Sammy.

image.altText: A seal sleeps on a rocky wall, looking towards the camera

items.title: MPs join calls for mayor in Melbourne to resign over character reference saga

children.content: The mayor of Wyndham City Council, Preet Singh, has faced protests and calls to quit after he wrote a character reference for a man convicted of child sex offences.

image.altText: A man in mayoral ceremonial robes poses for a professional portrait.

items.title: Autistic teenager handed supervision order for terror offences

children.content: A years-long saga that began with a 13-year-old being "radicalised" during a covert police operation is set to escalate to a Federal Court battle.

image.altText: Thomas's mother and father hold hands. Their faces are not seen in the photo

items.title: Team smashed by more than 1,100 points in four games forfeits season

children.content: A country Victorian football team that was beaten by a collective 1,101 points in four matches forfeits the remainder of the senior season.

image.altText: A scoreboard readds 313 to 1 as players walk off the field under grey skies

items.title: Child sex offender resigns as Melbourne Karen Buddhist group president

children.content: A convicted child sex offender who was told he was exempt from requiring a Working With Children Check has resigned as the president of a community organisation.

gallery.altText: An elderly man wearing a green jacket speaking into a microphone.

thumbnail.altText: An elderly man wearing a green jacket speaking into a microphone.

avatar.alt: A headshot of Nate standing in front of a dimly lit building wearing a jacket.

items.title: ISIS brides' arrest in Australia prompts anger in Syrian camp

image.altText: A woman in a black niqab walks along a row of tents, with children seen in the distance.

children.content: The arrest of three Australian women linked to the Islamic State group sparks anger and frustration inside the Syrian refugee camp where they had lived for years.

items.title: Teenager charged over home invasion linked to Melbourne arson attacks

children.content: Police say a shot was fired by intruders during an aggravated home invasion earlier this month, which is being linked to a series of arson attacks on licensed venues in Melbourne.

image.altText: A shattered window of a house.

items.title: Federal Court dismisses challenge to Victorian environmental project

children.content: A community group's argument that a delegate to the federal water minister made a legal error by approving the Nyah Floodplain Restoration Project has been rejected.

image.altText: A brunette woman crouching near a river speaks and gesticulates towards a group of five people

items.title: Swans move annual Pride Game away from St Kilda after Collard case

children.content: The fallout continues from the Lance Collard AFL tribunal case, with St Kilda cut from the annual Pride Game against Sydney.

gallery.altText: People in stands wearing white shirts and waving rainbow coloured Pride flags

thumbnail.altText: People in stands wearing white shirts and waving rainbow coloured Pride flags

items.title: New virus to control surging rabbit population at least 10 years away

children.content: Feral rabbits are causing damage on farms and in towns, but biological control research has been delayed by funding woes.

image.altText: A woman in a red jumper stands infront of a building labeled 'Bethanga cricket club'

items.title: Sexual abuse, a thwarted Olympic dream, and the sister who uncovered the truth

children.content: Ian Walker was one of the most promising athletes in the country until his life started to unravel, and the search for the truth in his tragic story has finally come to light.

image.altText: An older image of three young siblings arm in arm smiling at each other.

items.title: Voss exit shuts door on Carlton's premiership era that never was

children.content: Michael Voss leaves Carlton having taken the club to places it has rarely been in a quarter of a century, but still adrift from where it ultimately wants to be.

image.altText: Michael Voss with both fists raised in celebration

items.title: Sammy the seal believed dead on Mornington Peninsula

children.content: A beloved seal known as Sammy is believed to have died, after Zoos Victoria confirmed they were called to a rescue on Tuesday.

image.altText: A seal sleeps on a rocky wall, looking towards the camera

items.title: Emergency department nurse faces deportation due to son's condition

children.content: A Victorian emergency department nurse and her family face deportation before the end of the month due to her six-year-old son's developmental delay.

gallery.altText: A woman and man hold two young children.

thumbnail.altText: A woman and man hold two young children.

items.title: Man injured while allegedly blowing up ATM at Queen Victoria Market

children.content: Police say a man is under police guard in a Melbourne hospital after he allegedly blew up an ATM on Wednesday morning.

image.altText: An ATM in pieces.

items.title: May reveals 'mental breakdown' led to abrupt AFL retirement

children.content: Steven May opens up on his shock decision to retire from the AFL just two weeks before the season opener for Melbourne.

image.altText: AFL player preparing to catch the football

items.title: Property owner says he helped wounded officer after Dezi Freeman shot at police

children.content: A Porepunkah property owner says he helped a wounded police officer after the fatal shooting when Dezi Freeman killed two Victoria police officers.

image.altText: Frame of property owner being filmed on a phone camera

items.title: The pianist fighting the MSO over right to bring whole self to stage

children.content: In 2024, Jayson Gillham's show was cancelled for his on-stage comments about Palestine. Now he brings a legal suit and a new recital tour with a Palestinian Jordanian artist.

image.altText: Jayson Gillham, Australian man, 39, in a blazer and white shirt, concentrates as he plays piano in a concert hall.

items.title: Could cars save this struggling country mall?

children.content: A Victorian council is proposing to reopen a regional mall to vehicles after 44 years in an attempt to breathe new life into the area.

image.altText: A lease sign sits on a window

items.title: Farmers and environmentalists label federal budget 'savage' and 'tragic'

children.content: Weed and pest control programs have been cut in the federal budget as farmers and conservationists grapple with feral animals and noxious plants.

image.altText: Rabbits on a rural property

items.title: Police attend 160 family violence call-outs in Melbourne's south in one week

children.content: Police say a man who threatened to kill his wife with a hammer and tried to set fire to a property was just one of dozens of arrests for family violence in a single week in the Dandenong area.

image.altText: Unidentifiable man and woman arguing

items.title: Federal police charge man with using social media to threaten Victorian MP

children.content: A man will face court after federal police charged him following an investigation into a social media post that allegedly threatened a federal politician.

image.altText: The right arm of an unidentified person with an Australian Federal Police badge showing.

items.title: 'Portrait of anger' by Australian cartoonist wins $60,000 Stella Prize

children.content: Lee Lai's debut graphic novel, Stone Fruit, was longlisted for the Stella Prize in 2022. Four years on, she's won the prestigious award for her latest book, Cannon.

image.altText: A close-up photo of a person with short dark hair and glasses, smiling and wearing a tan checked shirt.

items.title: Diesel cars, wood fires contributing to pollution sending people to hospital

children.content: A new study has found a clear link between air pollution exposure and the risk of ending up in hospital. It's a battle Julia Ovens knows only too well.

image.altText: Julia stands on a balcony with a view of the city skyline behind her

items.title: The 'magnificent' mansion that housed movie stars, hippies and refugees

children.content: From post-war Jewish refugees, who arrived with nothing but hope, to the bohemian artists and performers who took over in the 1970s, historic Melbourne mansion Labassa has more stories than rooms.

gallery.altText: A wide picture showcases a historic Melbourne mansion, Labassa. The building is French Second Empire in style.
A grand staircase and ornate wallpaper show the grandeur of historic mansion Labassa.
A grand hallway filled with doors to partitioned flats. In the background is an ornate glass window.

thumbnail.altText: A wide picture showcases a historic Melbourne mansion, Labassa. The building is French Second Empire in style.
A grand staircase and ornate wallpaper show the grandeur of historic mansion Labassa.
A grand hallway filled with doors to partitioned flats. In the background is an ornate glass window.
